title_001: Should I Cut Them Off? — The Relationship Traffic-Light Test
meta_001: Pick the one person on your mind and read the relationship through 15 real behaviors — a traffic-light test for when to walk away.
html_004: that one person who keeps surfacing in your mind
html_006: and we light up a traffic signal on this bond right now.
html_008: Type their nickname, initials, or whatever you call them. This name shows up in the questions and the result.
html_010: 🔒 The name you type stays on this screen only — never saved or sent anywhere.
html_011: Just a fun relationship check-in. Where your heart goes is your call in the end.
html_029: 🧪 Dopamine Lab — more tests this way →
js_001: NAME suddenly cancels plans on me, or shows up late a lot.
js_005: When I’m going through something hard, NAME hears me out all the way.
js_009: With money (loans, splitting the bill), NAME quietly pushes the burden onto me.
js_013: NAME treats their own favors as a given, but sulks or gets hurt when I say no.
js_017: NAME talks trash about others a lot, or has passed my private stuff along.
js_021: After seeing NAME, I get home oddly drained and heavy-hearted.
js_025: When something goes well for me, NAME is genuinely happy for me.
js_029: NAME messes up and just moves on without a real apology.
js_033: NAME slips in little put-downs or belittling remarks about me.
js_037: NAME only reaches out when they need something, and ghosts or half-answers me otherwise.
js_039: Only calls when they need me
js_041: Even when I draw a line and say 「that’s a no」, NAME keeps pushing.
js_045: Around NAME, I can drop my guard and just be myself.
js_049: NAME compares me to others, or quietly dismisses my choices.
js_053: When I was really struggling, NAME has stood by and had my back.
js_057: Talks with NAME always orbit their story, and mine come second.
js_080: 💚 These are what keep this bond alive
js_083: Among your answers, these are the signals that wreck a bond when they repeat.
js_084: Still fixable — but worth keeping an eye on.
js_085: No big red-flag behavior, but the murky moments have been piling up.
js_088: The better the bond, the more expressing it grows it.
js_090: Don’t take them for granted — say 「I’m glad you’re here」 out loud once in a while.
js_092: Give back what you get — when they’re struggling, reach out first.
js_093: Keep checking in now and then
js_094: Even good bonds change. Don’t take today’s ease for granted — look back on it every so often.
js_095: 🟡 Before cutting off, 3 things to try first
js_096: There’s still room to change this before you end it.
js_098: Pick one thing that hurt and say 「that one really got to me」 as an I-statement. Their reaction is your answer.
js_100: Don’t bend to everything — turn down one draining favor with 「I can’t this time」 and watch how they take it.
js_102: Dial down contact for 2–3 weeks. Whether you feel lighter or actually miss them is the real signal.
js_103: 🔴 How to pull away, guilt-free, in 3 steps
js_104: Leaving doesn’t make you the bad one — it’s how you protect yourself.
js_106: 「This isn’t because I’m not enough — it’s because this bond is eating away at me.」 Guilt just proves you’re kind; it’s not a reason to stay.
js_108: Instead of a blowup, taper off contact and meetups. Reply slower and shorter; make no new plans.
js_110: Move the time and energy you spent on them toward people and activities that fill you up — so distance becomes recovery, not loss.
jst_004: this bond is worth keeping — you two bring out the good in each other.
jst_005: they’re not a bad person, but right now you two need some distance.
jst_006: — this bond is slowly wearing you down. It’s time to let go.

_app.title: Should I Cut Them Off?
_app.desc: Read that one person through 15 real behaviors — a walk-away traffic light.
